metadata.archetype_justification: Arrow is a pure computation library with no persistent state, no HTTP/RPC surface, no auth surface, no write operations, and no logging of user data. It exports functions and classes for date/time manipulation only.
metadata.context: Python library for human-friendly date and time handling.

readiness_profile.deployment_gate: Cleared for autonomous operation. Instrument observability. Define scope explicitly. Run controlled pilot first.

findings.title: Documented API Interface
findings.description: Arrow exposes a Python library API (import arrow) with comprehensive Sphinx documentation. No HTTP/REST/GraphQL/AsyncAPI interface exists — this is a library, not a service.
findings.gap: No network-level API interface (expected for a library)
findings.recommendation: None required. Consuming applications wrapping Arrow in a service are responsible for their own API documentation.

findings.title: Machine-Readable API Specification
findings.description: No HTTP/RPC surface — machine-readable spec is not applicable. Arrow's API contract is expressed via Python type hints, __all__ exports, and Sphinx documentation.

findings.title: Structured Error Responses
findings.description: No HTTP/RPC surface — structured error responses are not applicable. Arrow uses typed Python exceptions (ParserError).

findings.title: Idempotent Write Operations
findings.description: Read-only agents do not execute write operations. Arrow is a pure computation library with no write endpoints or state mutations.

ara_metadata.conditional_resolution: Resolved to INFO: agent_scope=read-only AND library has no write operations.
ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: Arrow is a pure computation library with no write endpoints. Read-only agent scope means idempotency is informational only.

findings.title: Structured Response Format
findings.description: Returns Python objects (Arrow instances). No serialization format concern for in-process library calls.

findings.title: Rate Limit Documentation and Headers
findings.description: No HTTP/RPC surface — rate limits are not applicable for an in-process library.

findings.title: Machine Identity Authentication
findings.description: Arrow is a computation library with no authentication surface. It does not authenticate callers. PyPI publishing uses GitHub Actions secrets appropriately.
findings.gap: No machine identity mechanism (expected — libraries are consumed, not called over network)

findings.title: Scoped Permissions (Least Privilege)
findings.description: No authorization model — all exported functions equally available. Expected for a computation library.

findings.title: Action-Level Authorization
findings.description: No actions requiring differential authorization. All operations are pure computations.

findings.title: Identity Propagation and Delegation
findings.description: No identity propagation surface. Arrow is invoked as a local function call with no network boundary.

findings.title: Credential Management
findings.description: PyPI API token managed via GitHub Actions secrets. No credentials hardcoded in source code.

findings.title: Immutable Audit Logging
findings.description: System does not execute agent-invoked write operations — audit logging is a consumer responsibility.

ara_metadata.conditional_resolution: Resolved to INFO: has_auth_surface=false AND has_write_operations=false.
ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: Library does not execute agent-invoked write operations. Audit logging is a consumer responsibility.

findings.title: Agent Identity Suspension
findings.description: System does not issue or enforce agent identities — suspension is a consumer responsibility.

findings.title: Compensation and Rollback
findings.description: System exposes no write operations — compensation logic is not applicable. Pure computation library.

ara_metadata.conditional_resolution: Resolved to INFO: has_write_operations=false AND has_http_rpc_surface=false. Archetype stateless-utility.
ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: Arrow has no write operations and no HTTP/RPC surface. Compensation logic is not applicable for a pure computation library.

findings.title: Concurrency Controls
findings.description: Arrow produces immutable Arrow instances — no shared mutable state. Thread-safe by design.

findings.title: Circuit Breakers and Resilience
findings.description: No external service dependencies. Arrow is self-contained with only local computation dependencies (python-dateutil, tzdata).
findings.gap: No circuit breakers (not needed — no external calls)

findings.title: Rate Limiting and Throttling
findings.description: No HTTP/RPC surface — rate limiting is not applicable for an in-process library.

findings.title: Blast Radius and Transaction Limits
findings.description: Read-only agents cannot modify records. Arrow has no state-mutating operations.

findings.title: Draft/Pending State
findings.description: Read-only agents do not make state changes. Arrow has no state mutation concepts.

findings.title: Configurable Approval Gates
findings.description: No operations require approval gates in a computation library.

findings.title: Sandbox/Staging Environment
findings.description: Libraries do not own staging environments. Arrow provides comprehensive local testing via tox/pytest with 99%+ coverage across 3 OS and 8 Python versions.
findings.gap: No staging environment (expected — testing is local for libraries)

findings.title: Sensitive Data Classification
findings.description: Stage A = No. Not a data-handling target — no PII/PHI/financial/credential data is stored, processed, or logged. Arrow operates exclusively on temporal values.

ara_metadata.conditional_resolution: Resolved to INFO: Stage A = No. Pure computation library (date/time) is not a data-handling target.
ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: Arrow operates exclusively on temporal values (dates, times, timestamps, timezones) which are not user-identifying data. No PII/PHI/financial/credential data is stored, processed, or logged.

b1.reasoning: Stage A = No. Not a data-handling target. B1 not evaluated.

b2.reasoning: Stage A = No. Not a data-handling target. B2 not evaluated.

b3.reasoning: Stage A = No. Not a data-handling target. B3 not evaluated.

findings.title: Data Residency and Sovereignty
findings.description: No persistent data store and no user-data logging — residency requirements do not apply. Arrow holds no data subject to residency constraints.

ara_metadata.conditional_resolution: Resolved to INFO: has_persistent_data_store=false AND has_logging_of_user_data=false.
ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: Arrow has no persistent data store and no user-data logging. It performs in-memory temporal computations only.

findings.title: Selective Query Support
findings.description: No query endpoints. Arrow returns bounded results (single Arrow instances, tuples, or bounded generators).

findings.title: Temporal Metadata and Freshness
findings.description: Arrow IS a temporal data library — it inherently provides timezone-aware datetime handling with UTC normalization. This is the library's core purpose.
findings.gap: None — temporal handling is the primary feature

findings.title: PII Redaction in Logs
findings.description: System does not log user data and holds no user data — PII-in-logs risk is not applicable. Arrow has no logging infrastructure.

findings.title: Data Quality Awareness
findings.description: Not applicable for a computation library. Arrow validates input data (raises ParserError for invalid formats) but does not maintain datasets requiring quality scores.

findings.title: Schema Versioning and API Contracts
findings.description: Arrow uses semantic versioning with tag-based releases. Well-defined __all__ export list. Type annotations with mypy strict mode. CHANGELOG.rst documents changes. No automated breaking-change detection in CI.
findings.gap: No automated API-breaking-change detection tool in CI pipeline
findings.recommendation: Consider adding griffe or pyright --verifytypes to CI for automated API compatibility checks.

findings.title: Semantically Meaningful Field Names
findings.description: Excellent naming conventions: humanize(), dehumanize(), shift(), floor(), ceil(), span(), replace(), format(), is_between(). No legacy abbreviations.

findings.title: Data Catalog / Metadata Layer
findings.description: Comprehensive Sphinx documentation on ReadTheDocs. API guides, getting-started guides, and release notes available.
findings.gap: No formal data catalog (not applicable for a computation library)

findings.title: Distributed Tracing and Structured Logging
findings.description: Library/utility — tracing and correlation are consumer concerns. Arrow has no logging or tracing instrumentation.

findings.title: Alerting on Error Rates and Latency
findings.description: Library/utility — alerting is a consumer concern. No API surface to monitor.

findings.title: Business Outcome Metrics
findings.description: Not applicable for a computation library. No business outcomes to measure at the library level.

evaluations.trigger: Service has operations >30s OR long-running workflows
Service has persistent state (stateful-crud, data-gateway, orchestrator)
Service is P0 priority OR is on the critical path

evaluations.reason: This is a library repository. This question does not apply.
